english: also sńags-pa (sngags pa), pf. bsńags (bsngags), fut. bsńag (bsngag), imp. sńog (sngog); to praise, commend, extol; to recommend; 'gró-bar sńags ('gro bar sngags) it is recommended to go Wdn.; bstod-sńag-pa (bstod sngag pa) to praise, to sing praises, frq.; sńag-(pa-)po (sngag (pa )po) a praiser, commender, Cs.; sńag-(par) 'os(-pa) (sngag (par) 'os (pa)), sńag-ldan (sngag ldan) praiseworthy; praised; also n. of the horse of Buddha Cs. — sńag-gsól (sngag gsol) praise, thanks.

1. incantation, magical formula, a set of words, consisting mostly of a number of unmeaning Sanskrit syllables, in the recital of which however perfect accuracy is requisite; hence detailed rules and instructions for a correct pronunciation of the Sanskrit sounds have been drawn up for Tibetan devotees. (On magical formulas v. Burn. II., 21, and note; on Buddhist magic in general v. Was. 142. 177, Kopp. II., 29.) — gzuńs-sńags (gzungs sngags), rig-sńags (rig sngags), and gsań-sńags (gsang sngags) prob. = sńags (sngags). — sńags sgrúb-pa (sngags sgrub pa), spél-ba (spel ba), zlá-ba (bzla ba), C. also *gyág-pa* (*gyag pa*), to recite, to pronounce charms, incantations; 'čáń-ba ('chang ba), 'dzín-pa ('dzin pa), to carry (charms) about one's self. — sńags-kyi t'ég-pa (sngags kyi theg pa) Tantrayāna, Mantrayāna, v. t'ég-pa (theg pa). — sńags-pa (sngags pa), sńags-mk'an (sngags mkhan), one versed in charms and their use, i.e. in orthodox and legitimate magic, as contained in the sacred books of religion. Opposed to this are ńan-sńags (ngan sngags), ńan-sńags-mk'an (ngan sngags mkhan), diabolical sorcerers and necromancers, and also common swindlers, jugglers, conjurers, fortune-tellers etc. — 2. praise, encomium Cs.
= dńańs (dngangs), v. dńań-ba (dngang ba) Glr., Pth.

for sńa (snga), sńon (sngon), e.g. sńan-čád (sngan chad), formerly, before, previously, beforehand, opp. to now Mil.; sńan-čád t'ó-tsams-pa bzód-par gsol (sngan chad tho 'tsams pa bzod par gsol) pardon our former scoffing Mil.; esp. W.: *ńán-la* (*ngan la*) for sńón-la (sngon la), sńar (sngar), before, previously; *ńán-ma* (*ngan ma*) for sńá-ma (snga ma), *ńán-me gyál-po* (*ngan me rgyal po*) the former or last king, *ńán-ma ńán-tar* (*ngan ma ngan tar*) just as before.

prop. sńá-ru (snga ru), before, beforehand, previously, formerly, at first; sńar de byás-pai 'óg-tu (sngar de byas pa'i 'og tu) not until that has been previously done Dzl.; sńar méd-pa (sngar med pa), sńar ma byás-pa (sngar ma byas pa), sńar ma skyés-pa (sngar ma skyes pa) what has not existed, or has not been done before, where we only say new, frq.; sńar lońs (sngar longs) get up first! Dzl.; sńár-bas kyań(lhag-pár) (sngar bas kyang(lhag par)) still more so than formerly, frq.; sńár-gyi (sngar gyi) what has been hitherto in use, frq.; sńár-gyi yi-ge rnyíń-pa-rnams (sngar gyi yi ge rnying pa rnams) the old writings of antiquity Glr.; sńar yin-na (sngar yin na) adv. = sńar (sngar) Mil.; sńar ltar (sngar ltar), sńar bźin (sngar bzhin) as before; sńár-nas (sngar nas) from before, from former times Mil.; also with reference to space: foremost, ahead, in advance, on, onward, joined to verbs of motion Dzl.; sńár-ba (sngar ba) the former, first-mentioned (?). In the sense of a postposition (c. accus.) sńar (sngar) is used but seldom, as far as I know only in spyan-sńár (spyan sngar).

a bolster, pillow, cushion; yo-byád sńás-su 'júg-pa (yo byad sngas su 'jug pa) Glr., C. col. *yo-jhé-la ńe čúg-pa* (*yo jhe la nge cug pa*), using the luggage as a pillow; sńas-stán (sngas stan), sńas-'ból (sngas 'bol), resp. dbu-sńás (dbu sngas) pillow; rgyab-sńás (rgyab sngas) a cushion for the back; sńas-mál (sngas mal) a couch constructed of pillows or cushions; sńas-'púńs (?) (sngas 'pungs) pillow, cushion Cs.; W. *nye* (*nye*) for *sńas* (*sngas*).

col. for sńon (sngon); sńún-la (sngun la) c. genit. before, ago, like gón-du (gong du); *dá-wa nyís-si ńún-la* (*da wa nyis si ngun la*) two months ago; *ńún-la soń* (*ngun la song*) he walked in advance, or ahead; *ńún-ma* (*ngun ma*) former, last; *ńún-ma-źag* (*ngun ma zhag*) W. two days before yesterday, *yan ńún-źag* (*yan ngun zhag*) three days before yesterday.
Lex., Cs.: a kind of pulse or pease; Sch. = món-sran (mon sran), v. greu (gre'u).
a root signifying blue or green; as sbst. plant, herb, vegetable, greens Mil.; sńo skyé-na (sngo skye na) when it is getting green or verdant. Comp. sńo-skyá (sngo skya) blue bice, pale blue, e.g.
